suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Explicitly instruct workers that when a feature must mirror an existing API, they should read the current source-of-truth interface file in addition to dependency files before implementing a Protocol or wrapper.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The cache-manager review noted the worker needed src/ui/state.py as the source-of-truth API surface; the current skill guidance emphasizes dependency files but not explicitly the live interface being mirrored.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add practical Windows file-creation guidance for large multiline files when structured edit tools are unavailable, so workers avoid wasting time on unsupported Create/Edit flows.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The json-repo-crud review and worker feedback both describe repeated failed Create/Edit attempts before falling back to PowerShell/Python file writes in this environment.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a note about adapting when Create/Edit-style tools are absent from the environment's actual tool surface, especially for refactor-worker missions that generate new files and tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The original cache-manager and json-repo-crud transcripts show repeated failed attempts to use unavailable Create/Edit tools before the workers found environment-compatible alternatives.

rejectedObservations.observation: Document that `Script.metadata.characters_mentioned` stores character names (not IDs), because cascade cleanup for character removal must match by name.
